mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) when FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, which reduces consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and business demand, which directly reduces corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in improved operational efficiency.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 60% of periods with positive INDPRO growth, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during expansionary macro environments.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 280, inflationary pressures force expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses 280, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing payroll growth and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness, which reduces aggregate income and corporate profit margins, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction model produces positive 60-day returns in 65% of cases, or the combined labor signal fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.04 in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher gross domestic product levels reflect stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings growth, which directly supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 levels (Rank IC < 0.01), or the signal reverses direction during periods of stable GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 100 baseline index signals a contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain disruptions, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in 60% of instances where INDPRO drops below 100, or the threshold signal loses predictive power (p > 0.05) during contractionary phases.
